insights.title: Tempo sync now auto-updates when host BPM changes
insights.summary: The AudioLooper implements TempoListener and recalculates the sync factor when the host tempo changes. Earlier versions lacked this, requiring a TransportHandler workaround.
insights.verified: fixed - AudioLooper.h:86 inherits TempoListener, tempoChanged calls setSyncMode

insights.title: Overlapping notes sync bug has been fixed
insights.summary: The syncFactor is now calculated per-voice in a loop, not just for voice 0. Earlier versions applied the same sync factor to all voices, causing double-speed playback when notes overlapped.
insights.verified: fixed - AudioLooper.cpp:545-576 iterates all voices for syncFactor

insights.title: Pitch detection on load is unreliable and often wrong by 10 semitones
insights.summary: The automatic root-note pitch detection that runs when a file is loaded frequently produces wrong results. David Healey reported a consistent 10-semitone offset. One user worked around it by commenting out the PitchDetection block in AudioLooper.cpp.

insights.title: RootNote stops auto-updating after first manual change
insights.summary: Once the RootNote parameter has been set to a value other than the default (64), the auto-detection on file load stops updating it. This appears to be intentional protection against overwriting preset values, but it surprises users who want detection to always run.
insights.verify_hint: grep RootNote in AudioLooper.cpp; look for conditional around setAttribute call in pitch detection path

insights.title: SyncMode max was 4 bars; 8/12/16 bar options added later
insights.summary: The original SyncMode enum only went up to 4 bars. Users with 8-bar and longer loops had no matching sync option. David Healey added 8, 12, and 16 bar options in a pull request. Older builds will not have these options.

insights.title: AudioLooper is a sound generator, not an effect — cannot be used in an FX plugin without enabling sound generators
insights.summary: Users trying to build an FX plugin with a loop player find the output is silent. The fix is to enable 'Enable Sound Generators FX' in Project Settings before building. David Healey also noted that a loop player plays audio rather than processing incoming audio, so an instrument plugin is the correct plugin type.

insights.title: Loop range and LoopEnabled are now saved in presets
insights.summary: The LoopEnabled state and loop start/end range are serialised in exportAsValueTree and restored in restoreFromValueTree. Earlier versions did not persist these values.
insights.verified: fixed - AudioLooper.cpp:389 saveAttribute(LoopEnabled); ProcessorInterfaces.cpp:776-777 saves loopStart/loopEnd

insights.title: Crash when SampleStartMod exceeds sample length for short files
insights.summary: If the SampleStartMod or manually set start position exceeds the number of samples in a file shorter than 44100 samples, HISE can crash. Users should clamp the start value or ensure loaded files meet a minimum length.
insights.verify_hint: grep SampleStartMod or setStartOffset in AudioLooper.cpp; check bounds on short buffers

insights.title: Use TransportHandler to fix tempo-sync drift on BPM change
insights.summary: Because the AudioLooper has no TempoListener, a TransportHandler callback can be used to re-apply the SyncMode attribute whenever the tempo changes, forcing the looper to recalculate its sync factor.

insights.title: Use Sampler instead of AudioLooper for complex sample-start control
insights.summary: Multiple trusted posters recommend switching from AudioLooper to the Sampler when fine-grained sample-start control is needed. The Sampler's SampleStart modulator maps 0-1 to the configured sample range and avoids several AudioLooper limitations around looping, preset saving, and pitch detection.

insights.title: setFile() works for loading external files, but path must be resolvable
insights.summary: AudioSampleProcessor.setFile() can load files from outside the project folder. Early confusion on the forum was resolved when David Healey confirmed it works correctly — the initial reports of failure were user error, not an API bug.

insights.title: getSampleLength() can be used to detect whether a file is loaded
insights.summary: Christoph Hart confirmed that getSampleLength() returns 0 when no file is loaded (with the edge case of a zero-length file also returning 0). It is the recommended way to check load status from script.

insights.title: setContentCallback fires on setFile(), but 'event' may be undefined
insights.summary: AudioFile.setContentCallback() does fire when a file is loaded via setFile(), but the event argument is not always defined. Guard with isDefined(event) before reading the new root note. David Healey confirmed this behaviour in 2023.
insights.verify_hint: grep setContentCallback in AudioSampleProcessor scripting; check when event is populated

insights.title: Trigger AudioLooper via Synth.playNote() from a script inside the module's MIDI chain
insights.summary: To create a play/stop button for an AudioLooper, place a ScriptProcessor inside the loop player's MIDI Processor chain and use Synth.playNote() / Synth.noteOffByEventId(). Events generated in a child chain only affect that generator, so no note filtering is needed.

insights.title: Use a Broadcaster attached to the RootNote parameter to react to auto-detection
insights.summary: Attach a Broadcaster to the AudioLooper's RootNote module parameter with bc.attachToModuleParameter() to receive callbacks when the pitch detector updates the value on file load. This is more reliable than polling.
